title: Essarâ€™s Green Mobility Targets 30,000 Clean Trucks, 100 Fuel-Charging Hubs in India
content: Essar Green Mobility is targeting to deploy 30000 electric and LNG trucks supported by a nationwide network of 100 alternative fuel and charging hubs across the country as part of Indiaâ€™s largest clean freight initiatives, marking a major step toward decarbonising the logistics sector by cutting around one million tonnes of CO2 annually from freight operations, according to the company.  B C Tripathi, Operating Partner Energy at Essar Group, said that the transition away from diesel will happen only when clean alternatives match the operational and economic realities of freight movement, highlighting the gap Essar aims to address through an integrated plan that brings together vehicle manufacturing, fleet operations and fuelling infrastructure to create a fully scaled clean freight ecosystem.  The platform combines Blue Energy Motors for truck production, GreenLine Mobility Solutions for fleet operations and Ultra Gas and Energy to build the supporting fuel and charging network, ensuring consistent vehicle supply, fuel availability and operational efficiencies that have historically slowed wider uptake of clean freight solutions in Indiaâ€™s carbon intensive logistics sector.  GreenLine currently operates more than 800 LNG powered trucks across sectors including steel, cement, FMCG and chemicals, covering over 70 million kilometres and reducing 19756 tonnes of carbon emissions, equivalent to saving nearly 790262 trees, with each 55 tonne truck able to travel about 1200 kilometres on a single tank.  An equity round totaling USD 275 million, including a USD 20 million investment from Zerodha co founder Nikhil Kamath, will accelerate deployment of over 10000 new LNG and EV trucks and support the rollout of 100 LNG refuelling stations, EV charging points and battery swapping facilities, with Essar Director Anshuman Ruia calling it a game changing shift in Indiaâ€™s heavy trucking industry.
author: Analysts Account
